Off Calendar Jan-05-2026 Off Calendar

REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jul-02-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Joseph Fenton appeared via zoom (pro per) (petitioner).

Mr. Fenton stated that there was a creditor's claim filed, that the creditor negotiated this claim with the heirs, that they are dealing with some tax issues, and that the administrator holds assets of $532,000.00. The matter is ordered off calendar. The status on close of estate is set for January 5, 2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Joseph Fenton (administrator) appeared via zoom.
Guy Noyes appeared via zoom for Cynthia Scully (surviving spouse).
Elizabeth Falk appeared via zoom (pro/per).

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar Feb-26-2024 Continued To Mar-27-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Jan-30-2023 Text Ruling

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jan-30-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Elizabeth Falk (Petitioner/Objector) in propria persona, in court. Christina Tanti, Esq., via Zoom, for and with Cynthia Scully (Petitioner), via Zoom. Joseph F. Fenton (Proposed Third Party Administrator) in propria persona, via Zoom. John Scully (Decedent's Father) in propria persona, in court.

The Court stated that before it today were competing petitions for letters of administration one filed by Cynthia Scully and one filed by Elizabeth Falk, that it had heard this matter once before, that it is an unfortunate situation that brings us to this point, that under the probate code that Ms. Scully has priority and/or a higher position as the surviving spouse even if there was a separation, that the question is whether or not that there is good cause not to grant that petition, that there is an issue with fairness here, and that the Court was glad to see a joint presentation by the parties that the children were going to be the top priority during this trying situation. The parties provided extensive argument in support of their respective petitions/positions. Ms. Falk presented her concerns with the Court appointing a fiduciary that has never done a probate administration and that she also had concerns as to how Ms. McDermott would handle the community property designations. The Court inquired if Ms. McDermott had ever done a probate. Ms. Tanti responded stating that she could not speak to the Court's inquiry, that Ms. McDermott has acted as a professional in a conservatorship capacity, and that she could file a supplemental declaration. Ms. Falk stated that Ms. McDermott had told her directly that she had never done a probate and that's why she filed her declaration reflecting that statement. The Court stated that it will decide the community property. Ms. Tanti apologized to the Court for not being able to confirm that Ms. McDermott had done an estate administration, argued that her client has priority, that her client doesn't object to Mr. Fenton, that the issue is that her client wants a licensed fiduciary with a separate attorney, and that saving fees should not be the reason why an attorney is appointed. The Court stated that when an attorney is appointed that their obligations are more robust. Mr. Fenton confirmed for the Court that he has been an administrator before as well as a guardian ad litem in Alameda County, etc. The Court stated that it does not want additional costs at the outset and that it is thinking about the minors.

At the conclusion of argument/comments, the Court granted Elizabeth Falk's Amended Petition for Letters of Administration. James Fenton is appointed administrator with full authority. Bond is set at $586,000.00. Isidoor Bornstein is appointed probate referee. Order for the same signed in open court. Status on close of estate is set for February 26, 2024, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. The Court addressed Ms. Scully stating that she shouldn't view this as a loss and that Mr. Fenton will be an adminstrator that is fully bonded. The Court concluded the hearing by commending everyone for their efforts.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Singh. Matter not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-17-2022 Continued To Jan-09-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nicole R. Takemoto, via Zoom, for and with Elizabeth Falk, in court, Petitioner; Nathan Pastor, via Zoom, for and with Cynthia Scully, Objector.

The court mentions Cynthia Scully's objection and competing petition, pending in the court's e-filing queue. Ms. Takemoto acknowledges receipt of the preceding yesterday. The court acknowledges the nomination of a third-party to serve as Administrator and of overall respectful tone of the competing petitions and of the court's interest in preserving the minor children's positions in this matter. The court and the parties discuss the importance of meeting and conferring and of the possibility of appointing a guardian ad litem to represent the minors.

After additional discussion, the Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to Jan-09-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The court advises the parties that it will be instructing the filing clerks to schedule Cynthia Scully's competing Petition for Letters of Administration for the same date and time stated above.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Cristobal. Not Reported.
